title: Contact Ring Platform
description: Questions about the open-source codebase, this community deployment, self-hosting, or building on Ring — write to the team behind ring-platform.org. We read every message.
faqWhatIsAnswer: Ring Platform is an open-source, white-label stack for professional communities: verified organization profiles (Entities), tiered Opportunities from public listings to confidential rooms, a multi-vendor Store with WayForPay and wallet rails, real-time Messenger, News, and AI-assisted opportunity matching — built on React 19, Next.js 16, Auth.js v5, and a PostgreSQL-first data layer. Clone it from GitHub, shape it with ring-config and locales, and deploy on your own infrastructure. ring-platform.org is the community portal for contributors and self-hosters; managed hosting and Legiox-guided ringization live at Ringdom.
faqGetStartedAnswer: On this site: register, complete your profile, then explore Entities and Opportunities or open the Store. As a developer: clone github.com/connectplatform/ring, copy ring-config.template.json to ring-config.json, run install.sh, and follow /docs/getting-started for a local stack. Planning a production clone or need architecture advice? Say so in the form — we will point you to the right doc or path.
faqResponseTimeAnswer: Most messages receive a reply within two business days. For account, order, or wallet issues, use the email on your profile so we can verify you quickly. Security or data-integrity reports: put “security” in the subject — those are prioritized.
faqInquiries: What can I contact you about?
faqInquiriesAnswer: Using this deployment (profiles, opportunities, store, membership, notifications); bugs and documentation gaps in the OSS repo; self-hosting, DB_BACKEND_MODE, and customization; contributing code or translations; vendor onboarding on this marketplace; partnerships and Ringdom settler / enterprise hosting (we route to ringdom.org when that is the right fit).

sidebar.platformInfoDesc: The reference open-source community, opportunity, and marketplace platform — documentation and living demo for Ring self-hosters worldwide.
sidebar.needHelpDesc: Most answers are in /docs. For anything else, use the form on this page.
